assertion_results.text: Documentation includes all four HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
assertion_results.evidence: All four methods present: GET /api/users, GET /api/users/:id, POST /api/users, PUT /api/users/:id, DELETE /api/users/:id

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint has a human-readable description explaining what it does
assertion_results.evidence: Descriptions are minimal one-liners like 'Returns a list of users' and 'Creates a new user' - not explanatory descriptions of behavior and purpose

assertion_results.text: Path parameters (e.g., :id) are documented with their type and constraints
assertion_results.evidence: Path parameter :id is used in routes but never documented with type (ObjectId/MongoId) or constraints

assertion_results.text: Query parameters for the list endpoint (page, limit) are documented with defaults
assertion_results.evidence: Query params mentioned as 'page and limit query params' but no table, no types, no defaults, no constraints documented

assertion_results.text: POST and PUT endpoints have request body schemas with field types and validation rules
assertion_results.evidence: POST has a bullet list of fields with types but no validation rules (min/max length, format). PUT says 'Same fields as POST but all optional' with no detail.

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint includes at least one response example with realistic JSON data
assertion_results.evidence: GET /api/users has a response example but uses '...' placeholders. GET /:id uses '...' for all values. POST and PUT have no response examples.

assertion_results.text: Error responses are documented with status codes (400, 404, 409, 422) and response bodies
assertion_results.evidence: Only mentions '404 if user not found' and '409 if email already exists' as inline text. No error response bodies, no 400 or 422 documentation.

assertion_results.text: An OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec is produced with valid paths and component schemas
assertion_results.evidence: No OpenAPI JSON file was produced. Only a Markdown file was generated.
